story.soThat: code quality rules are enforced consistently
story.tasks: - Task 1: Create .eslintrc.js configuration file (AC: #1, #2, #3) - Subtask 1.1: Install required dependencies (eslint, @typescript-eslint/parser, @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin, eslint-plugin-prettier, eslint-config-prettier) - Subtask 1.2: Create .eslintrc.js with required extends - Subtask 1.3: Configure NestJS best practices rules - Subtask 1.4: Configure TypeScript strict rules - Subtask 1.5: Configure no-console rule for production - Subtask 1.6: Configure import order rules - Subtask 1.7: Configure no-unused-vars rule - Task 2: Add npm scripts to package.json (AC: #4) - Subtask 2.1: Add "lint" script with --fix flag - Subtask 2.2: Test lint script execution - Task 3: Configure VS Code integration (AC: #5) - Subtask 3.1: Create/update .vscode/settings.json - Subtask 3.2: Enable eslint.autoFixOnSave - Subtask 3.3: Enable editor.codeActionsOnSave for ESLint - Task 4: Integrate lint check in CI/CD pipeline (AC: #6) - Subtask 4.1: Add lint check stage to ci-pipeline.yml (Epic 11-2) - Subtask 4.2: Add lint check stage to cd-staging.yml (Epic 11-3) - Subtask 4.3: Add lint check stage to cd-production.yml (Epic 11-4) - Subtask 4.4: Configure to fail build on violations

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. `.eslintrc.js` oluşturulmuş 2. Extends: - `@typescript-eslint/recommended` - `plugin:@typescript-eslint/recommended` - `plugin:prettier/recommended` 3. Rules: - NestJS best practices - TypeScript strict rules - No console.log in production - Consistent import order - No unused vars 4. `package.json`: `"lint": "eslint \"{src,apps,libs,test}/**/*.ts\" --fix"` 5. VS Code integration: `.vscode/settings.json` (eslint.autoFixOnSave) 6. CI/CD: Lint check mandatory

doc.title: Epic 12 Technical Specification - Code Quality & Standards
doc.snippet: .eslintrc.js structure and configuration contract with TypeScript-first approach, Prettier integration, and NestJS framework-specific rules.

doc.title: Epic 12 - Code Quality & Standards
doc.snippet: Complete story definition, acceptance criteria, and dependencies. Specifies @typescript-eslint/recommended extends and required rules configuration.

doc.title: PRD - Non-Functional Requirements: Coding Standards
doc.snippet: Production-tested naming conventions from hrsync-backend: kebab-case files/folders, DTO patterns, entity naming standards.

doc.title: Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: 70% minimum coverage requirement enforced by CI/CD. Unit test patterns with Arrange-Act-Assert, integration tests, and E2E test structure.

codeEntry.reason: TypeScript strict mode already enabled (strict: true) - ESLint must align with these settings

ESLint dependencies already installed: eslint@^9.18.0, @eslint/js@^9.18.0, @eslint/eslintrc@^3.2.0, typescript-eslint@^8.20.0, eslint-config-prettier@^10.0.1, eslint-plugin-prettier@^5.2.2. Lint script already configured on line 15.
Sample NestJS application entry point - should be checked by ESLint for best practices compliance

constraints.constraint: TypeScript-first approach with @typescript-eslint integration required for all lint rules
Prettier integration mandatory via eslint-config-prettier to prevent rule conflicts
TypeScript strict mode compatibility required (tsconfig.json already has strict: true)
NestJS framework-specific rules and best practices must be enforced
CI/CD integration mandatory - lint check must fail build on violations (Epic 11)
VS Code integration required for developer experience (format on save, auto-fix)
ESLint check must complete in under 2 seconds for projects ≤10,000 LOC
No console.log statements allowed in production code
Import order must be consistent (external packages → DTOs → Services → Repositories → Entities → Interfaces → Enums → Events)
Unused variables must be detected and reported

interface.name: GitHub Actions: Staging CD
interface.signature: cd-staging.yml with lint check stage before deployment

interface.name: GitHub Actions: Production CD
interface.signature: cd-production.yml with lint check stage before deployment

tests.standards: Jest testing framework with 70% minimum coverage enforced by CI/CD. Unit tests follow Arrange-Act-Assert pattern. Integration tests for database and API endpoints. E2E tests for critical user journeys. ESLint should validate test file quality and consistency.
tests.locations: - test/ (E2E tests) - src/**/*.(spec|test).ts (Unit tests) - test/e2e/ (E2E test files) - test/integration/ (Integration tests)

testIdea.scenario: ESLint configuration file exists and is valid
testIdea.steps: 1. Create .eslintrc.js with required extends 2. Verify file is valid JSON/JavaScript 3. Run eslint --print-config to validate configuration
1. Check .eslintrc.js contains @typescript-eslint/recommended 2. Check .eslintrc.js contains plugin:@typescript-eslint/recommended 3. Check .eslintrc.js contains plugin:prettier/recommended
1. Run npm run lint 2. Verify command completes without errors on clean code 3. Verify --fix flag applies auto-fixes

testIdea.scenario: VS Code integration enables auto-fix on save
testIdea.steps: 1. Create intentional violation (unused variable) 2. Save file 3. Verify auto-fix is applied or violation is highlighted

testIdea.scenario: CI/CD pipeline fails build on lint violations
testIdea.steps: 1. Introduce lint violation 2. Push to feature branch 3. Verify CI pipeline fails on lint check 4. Fix violation and verify pipeline passes
